subject: Mathematics (NCERT - Ganita Prakash)
chapter: Connecting the Dots (Geometry - Lines and Angles)

activities_projects.title: Parallel Line Angle Tester
activities_projects.objective: Verify the equality of corresponding angles
steps: Draw two parallel lines $l$ and $m$ using a scale
Draw a transversal $n$ intersecting both lines
Measure a pair of corresponding angles
Check if they are equal and repeat for all four pairs
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Demonstrates that corresponding angles are equal when lines are parallel

activities_projects.title: The 'Z' Angle Investigation
steps: Draw a transversal crossing two parallel lines
Highlight the 'Z' shape formed by the lines
Trace one alternate interior angle and see if it fits perfectly over the other
Write the property: 'Alternate interior angles are ___'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies and proves the equality of alternate interior angles
activities_projects.evaluation: Clear tracing and correct conclusion (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Interior Angles on the Same Side
activities_projects.objective: Verify the supplementary nature of co-interior angles
steps: Draw two parallel lines and a transversal
Measure the two interior angles on the left side of the transversal
Find their sum
Repeat for the right side and check if both sums equal $180^{\circ}$
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that co-interior angles are supplementary
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct summation of measured angles (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Adjustable Alphabet
activities_projects.objective: Analyze how angles change when lines are not parallel
steps: Create a 'Z' shape with three sticks and two thumbtacks
Tilt the sticks so the top and bottom are NOT parallel
Measure the interior angles and discuss why they are no longer equal
Explain the condition needed for the angles to match
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that parallel lines are a requirement for angle equality

activities_projects.title: Linear Pair vs. Vertically Opposite
activities_projects.objective: Compare two types of angle relationships
steps: Cross two pencils to form four angles
Measure two adjacent angles (Linear Pair) and add them
Measure two opposite angles and compare them
Tabulate the differences in their properties
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Distinguishes between supplementary and equal angle relationships

activities_projects.title: The Exterior Angle Theorem Proof
activities_projects.objective: Verify that an exterior angle equals the sum of interior opposites
steps: Cut out a triangle and extend one side to create an exterior angle
Cut out the two opposite interior angles
Place the two cut-outs inside the exterior angle to see if they fill it completely
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visually proves the exterior angle property of a triangle
activities_projects.evaluation: Successful placement and fit of angles (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Shadow Geometry
steps: Stand two pencils vertically on a flat surface
Measure the angles their shadows make with the pencils
Explain why the shadows are parallel if the light source is far away (like the Sun)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates parallel lines to light and shadows

activities_projects.title: Checking for Parallelism
activities_projects.objective: Use angles to determine if lines are parallel
materials: Unlabeled diagrams of lines and transversals
steps: Look at a diagram where corresponding angles are marked as $70^{\circ}$ and $72^{\circ}$
Decide if the lines are parallel or not
State the 'Converse Property' used to make your decision
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Uses the converse of angle properties to test parallelism
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct decision and naming of the property (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Angle Sum Challenge: Quadrilaterals
steps: Draw any quadrilateral
Draw one diagonal to split it into two triangles
Calculate the total angle sum of the quadrilateral based on triangle properties ($180^{\circ} + 180^{\circ}$)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects simple shapes to solve complex geometric sums

activities_projects.title: The Yoga Angle Chart
activities_projects.objective: Identify angles in human body postures
steps: Trace the lines formed by the arms, legs, and torso in a pose
Identify at least one pair of supplementary angles or a transversal
Estimate the measures of the angles formed
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies geometry to human anatomy and movement
activities_projects.evaluation: Creative identification of geometric elements (10 marks)
